title: NYC Central Park Stabbings Investigated as Possible Hate Crimes
summary: New York City police arrested a man in connection with two stabbings on Manhattan’s Upper West Side Thursday afternoon and are evaluating whether the attacks amount to hate crimes, Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch said.

title: Eli Lilly-Backed Scribe Raises $128.7 Million in Upsized IPO
summary: Scribe Therapeutics Inc., a clinical-stage developer of gene therapies for heart diseases, raised $128.7 million in an upsized initial public offering priced at the top of its marketed range.

title: ASX’s Finance Chief Tobin Flags His Intention to Retire
summary: ASX Ltd.’s chief financial officer Andrew Tobin intends to retire after four years with Australia’s main exchange.

title: 7-Eleven Owner Taps Ex-Dr Pepper Executive to Lead US Business
summary: Seven & i Holdings Co. appointed former Keurig Dr Pepper Inc. executive Mauricio Leyva to lead its North American convenience-store business, putting a consumer industry veteran in charge as the Japanese retailer tries to revive performance and prepare the unit for an eventual public listing.

title: Japan 40-Year Yield Surges as Traders Question BOJ on Inflation
summary: Japan’s 40-year government bond yield led gains across maturities, underscoring concerns that the nation’s central bank isn’t tightening policy fast enough to quell inflation.

title: Japan Banks With Shipping Finance Expertise Are Latest to Merge
summary: A proposed merger between Iyogin Holdings Inc. and Ehime Bank Ltd. adds to a series of recent M&A activity between Japanese regional lenders as an aging and shrinking population weighs on their business.

title: India’s Biggest IPO of 2026 Briefly Dips Below Offer Price
summary: SBI Funds Management Ltd.’s shares briefly fell below their offer price just four days after their trading debut, signaling weak secondary-market demand for the nation’s largest asset manager.

title: Japanese Shoppers Are Driving Up Jewelry Sales to a Record
summary: Japanese shoppers are driving up luxury jewelry sales to a record as consumers seek a safe haven from inflation and the weakening yen.

title: Modi Seeks to Calm Anger as India Student Protests Widen
summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi made a fresh bid to calm mounting anger over examination paper leaks as students began nationwide protests on Friday, with demands growing for the education minister to step down.

title: China Rushes to Buy East Russian Oil as Mideast Risks Grow
summary: Chinese buyers are snapping up Russia’s flagship ESPO crude weeks earlier than usual, as heightened risks to Middle East oil flows prompt refiners to secure supplies before they’re needed.

title: Indonesia Seeks to Revive Cocoa Output After Years of Decline
summary: Once the world’s third-largest cocoa producer, Indonesia is seeking to revive cocoa production after years of underinvestment, aging trees and pests left the industry short of beans and increasingly dependent on imports.

title: Global Bonds Are Reeling as Oil Surge Renews Inflation Threat
summary: Global bonds are being pummeled by the latest resurgence in energy prices, delivering losses to investors who bet the worst of this year’s rout was over and teeing up credibility tests for central bankers.

title: Dangote Prefers London Over Dubai for Listing of Cement Unit
summary: Dangote Cement Plc prefers to list its cement business in London because it will be quicker to sell shares on the exchange in the UK than in Dubai, according to Mariya Dangote, a board member in the cement business.

title: Stock Slide Extends on AI Jitters, Oil Near $100: Markets Wrap
summary: A global selloff in technology stocks gained momentum as doubts over returns from billions of dollars spent on artificial intelligence were compounded by a surge in oil prices that rekindled inflation concerns.

title: Tanker Exits Red Sea Dark as Another China Ship Heads to Strait
summary: A Greek tanker carrying Saudi crude exited the Red Sea with its transponder off, while another Chinese vessel headed toward Bab el-Mandeb as Houthi attacks heightened risks for commercial shipping.

title: FTSE 100 Live: UK Stocks Fall as Oil Stocks Drag
Overseas Buyers In Hot Pursuit of US Crude as Wars Escalate
summary: US crude is drawing overseas interest from Asian and European refiners as supply concerns spread from the Middle East to the Black Sea.

title: UK Pub Stocks Fall as Heat Wave Proves Too Hot For Football Fans
summary: UK pub stocks slumped this week as a sales boost from the World Cup failed to offset the drag from a brutal heat wave and higher costs.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
summary: Brent crude traded near $100 a barrel after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea opened a new front in the Middle East conflict, while President Donald Trump threatened to extend US strikes on Iran.

title: Japan’s Katayama Says to Take Bold FX Steps Decisively as Needed
summary: Japanese authorities will take bold steps to counter moves in the currency market decisively as needed, and a US Treasury report issued overnight underscores a shared view that excessive currency moves are undesirable, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said.

title: Gold Extends Decline as Higher Energy Costs Raise Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Gold extended a retreat as the widening conflict in the Middle East lifted energy prices and increased expectations the US Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.

title: PBOC Adds Most Liquidity to Economy in Five Months to Aid Growth
summary: China’s central bank made its biggest addition of liquidity to the economy in five months via its medium-term lending facility to support growth ahead of its late July Politburo meeting and to accommodate government bond issuance.

title: Korean Stocks Sink as Chipmakers Drop on Middle East, China IPO
summary: South Korean stocks fell as funds cut exposure to chipmakers after overnight losses in US peers, while factoring in a Chinese rival’s imminent share sale and rising Middle East tensions.

title: Metals Fluctuate as Middle East Escalation Keep Bulls in Check
summary: Base metals fluctuated at the end of the week as the threat of an escalating conflict in the Middle East — and more turbulence for the global economy — kept a lid on optimism.
